paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human; macrophage cell line (myeloid), no T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: covalent ligand... degrades both IRF5 and IRF8, inhibiting their pro-inflammatory activity
paper_assessments.rationale: Chemical degrader study in macrophages targeting innate pro-inflammatory IRF signalling; no T cell or TCR readout.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary CD4+ T cells; Th17 subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 as a novel intrinsic transcriptional inhibitor of TH17-cell differentiation
paper_assessments.rationale: T-cell-intrinsic knockout study, but phenotype is Th17 lineage differentiation, not proximal TCR signalling or an inhibitory TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 aggravates NAFLD by promoting lipid accumulation through the BMAL1/PPARγ axis
paper_assessments.rationale: Hepatic lipid metabolism study in non-immune tissue; no T cell or TCR involvement.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and non-human; myeloid cells (MDSCs) and epithelial cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8's silencing promotes immune suppression... increasing myeloid-derived suppressor cells
paper_assessments.rationale: Review; IRF8 acts in myeloid/epithelial cells and loss promotes MDSC-mediated T cell suppression, not TCR-intrinsic inhibition. Reviews cannot support strong/moderate.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 primarily regulates cDC1 and indirectly neutrophil functions
paper_assessments.rationale: IRF8 role in dendritic cell/neutrophil function in kidney injury; T-cell priming only mentioned indirectly, no TCR inhibition readout.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse); primary CD4+ T cells; Th9 subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 complexes boost the Th9 program and repress Il4 expression
paper_assessments.rationale: T-cell-intrinsic differentiation study of Th9 program and anti-tumour function; addresses lineage specification, not TCR inhibition or an inhibitory TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human; bulk tissue deconvolution (mixed immune cells, incl. Tregs/CD8)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: T cells CD8, and T cells regulatory (Tregs) were shown to be negatively linked with IRF8
paper_assessments.rationale: Bioinformatics/ML expression-correlation study in orbital inflammation; associative only, no experimental TCR functional readout.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human (mouse) and human; tumor cells (non-T-cell), CTL-mediated killing
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 represses p53, maintaining tumor cell sensitivity to ferroptosis and CTL-mediated killing
paper_assessments.rationale: IRF8 acts intrinsically in tumor cells regulating ferroptosis/CTL susceptibility; no perturbation of T cell TCR signalling itself.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IRF8 in microglia plays a major role in controlling anxiety in a sex dependent manner
paper_assessments.rationale: Neurobehavioural study of microglial IRF8; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and non-human (mouse xenograft); B-cell lymphoma cells (non-T-cell)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Loss of IRF8 suppressed proliferation by decreasing p38 and ERK phosphorylation
paper_assessments.rationale: B-cell lymphoma proliferation study; IRF8 acts intrinsically in B lymphoma cells, no T cell TCR readout.

parsed.tier_rationale: No retrieved primary study provides direct causal, indirect mechanistic, or even associative evidence linking IRF8 to proximal TCR signalling inhibition, an inhibitory checkpoint response, TCR-linked anergy/hyporesponsiveness, or exhaustion with demonstrated impaired TCR function. The only T-cell-intrinsic primary studies (PMC3112536, PMC5727025) concern Th17/Th9 lineage differentiation, which the rubric classes as weak-tier (differentiation/lineage specification). Remaining evidence is non-T-cell (myeloid, hepatocyte, tumor, B-cell, microglia), a review (PMC9454819), or an expression-correlation computational study (PMC11190126). Under the mandatory rules, differentiation and expression correlations are insufficient for moderate, and reviews/computational inference cannot raise the tier; the presence of T-cell differentiation primary studies supports weak over none.
